L1.area: Image decoding and codec processing layer

L2.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists in an image decoding routine where the buffer size for tile data is incorrectly calculated by assuming a fixed component width instead of accounting for varying per-component sizes. A crafted image with mismatched component counts can trigger the out-of-bounds read.

L3.area: JPEG 2000 image decoding and tile processing logic
L3.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists in the JPEG 2000 image decoding routine where the buffer size for tile data is incorrectly calculated by assuming a fixed component width instead of accounting for varying per-component sizes. A crafted JPEG 2000 image with mismatched component counts can cause an out-of-bounds read during tile data unpacking.

ground_truth.reason: The JPEG 2000 tile decoding routine incorrectly calculates the buffer size using a fixed component width assumption rather than summing individual component precisions, and fails to validate that the tile component count matches the image component count, leading to an out-of-bounds read.
